result: I’d erase the inherited scaffolding of scarcity—make it so no child is born into a world where their possibilities are pre-trimmed by poverty, caste, zip code, gender, or pigment. Not by redistributing crumbs, but by deleting the very code that lets one womb be a launching pad and another a debt collector. A single edit: every newborn inherits, as a birthright, the maximum bandwidth of human potential—nutrition, shelter, learning, safety, love—automatically, no questions asked. After that, let the chaos of talent and obsession run wild.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
